title: MongoDB Atlas Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**MongoDB Atlas** is the managed-MongoDB SaaS from MongoDB, Inc. Its **Admin API v2** (control plane) lives at `https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2`. This connector — `MongoDBAtlasConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "mongodb_atlas"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs to manage their Atlas footprint:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Organizations | `/orgs` | List + read |
| Projects (Groups) | `/groups` | List, read, create, delete |
| Clusters | `/groups/{id}/clusters` | List, read, create, modify (PATCH), delete |
| Database Users | `/groups/{id}/databaseUsers` | List + SCRAM user CRUD |
| Network Access List | `/groups/{id}/accessList` | List + add IP/CIDR/AWS security group entries |
| Cloud Backup Snapshots | `/groups/{id}/clusters/{name}/backup/snapshots` | List |
| Alerts | `/groups/{id}/alerts` | List + filter by status |
| Programmatic API Keys | `/orgs/{id}/apiKeys` | List (read-only) |
| Billing | `/orgs/{id}/invoices` | List invoices |

The connector retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts) honouring `Retry-After`, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::MongoDBAtlasHTTPClient`). The MongoDB **wire protocol** (data plane, `mongodb://`) is out of scope — this is an admin-plane connector. Tenants that want alerts or cluster descriptions in their KB can use `helpers.normalizer.normalize_alert` / `normalize_cluster` directly.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create an Atlas Programmatic API Key

1. Sign in to https://cloud.mongodb.com.
2. Pick the **Organization** you want this connector to manage.
3. **Access Manager → API Keys → Create API Key**.
4. Give it a descriptive name (e.g. `shielva-atlas-connector`).
5. Grant roles — for full management capability choose **Organization Owner**, or **Organization Project Creator** + per-project **Project Owner** as a tighter alternative.
6. Click **Next**. Atlas shows you the **Public Key** and **Private Key**.
7. **Copy the private key now** — Atlas only displays it once.

### 2. Whitelist the caller IP

On the same API Key page, scroll to **Access List** and add the egress IP(s) of the host that will run the connector (or `0.0.0.0/0` for unrestricted — discouraged in production).

### 3. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → MongoDB Atlas**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `public_key` (string) | Atlas public key (8-char) |
| `private_key` (secret) | Atlas private key (UUID-shaped) |
| `default_org_id` (string, optional) | 24-char hex from URL |
| `default_project_id` (string, optional) | 24-char hex from URL |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2` (rarely needed) |
| `api_version` (string, optional) | Default `2025-03-12` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 100 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `public_key` + `private_key` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `MongoDBAtlasHTTPClient` with `httpx.DigestAuth`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 4. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists 1 org as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
MongoDB Atlas Admin API uses **HTTP Digest authentication** (RFC 7616) with a `(public_key, private_key)` pair issued from **Organization → Access Manager → API Keys** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry until the key is rotated.

### Credential model

- `public_key` is the Digest username.
- `private_key` is the Digest password.
- `httpx.DigestAuth(public_key, private_key)` handles the 401-challenge → 200-success two-round-trip dance natively — no manual nonce/HA1/HA2 work needed.

### Versioned Accept header

Atlas REQUIRES a versioned media-type in the `Accept` header:

```
Accept: application/vnd.atlas.<YYYY-MM-DD>+json
```

The connector defaults to `2025-03-12` and exposes it as the `api_version` install field for future bumps.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `public_key` + `private_key`. Initialises `MongoDBAtlasHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Atlas endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns an empty `TokenInfo(token_type="digest")` — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /orgs?itemsPerPage=1` — minimal Digest probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — Digest has no token lifecycle. |
| token storage | None. Credentials live in `self.config` and are re-read at request time. |

### Wire contract

Every outbound request:

```
Accept:        application/vnd.atlas.2025-03-12+json
Content-Type:  application/json
Authorization: Digest username=<public_key>, realm="MongoDB Atlas", nonce=…, uri=…, response=…, qop=auth, nc=…, cnonce=…
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `public_key` | string | yes | `self.public_key` | Digest username |
| `private_key` | secret | yes | `self.private_key` | Digest password |
| `default_org_id` | string | no | `self.default_org_id` | Per-call default org for shorthand ops |
| `default_project_id` | string | no | `self.default_project_id` | Per-call default project for shorthand ops |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2`) | Override for private Atlas deployments |
| `api_version` | string | no | `self.api_version` (default `2025-03-12`) | Versioned Accept header |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["public_key", "private_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2"
_DEFAULT_API_VERSION = "2025-03-12"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `MongoDBAtlasConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::MongoDBAtlasHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `public_key` + `private_key`; initialise `MongoDBAtlasHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for Digest auth — returns an empty `TokenInfo(token_type="digest")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 org via `GET /orgs?itemsPerPage=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Atlas admin API is a control plane — no documents to ingest. Returns `SyncResult(COMPLETED, 0, 0, 0)`. Callers that want alerts/clusters projected into a KB should use `helpers.normalizer.normalize_alert` / `normalize_cluster` directly.

children.title: Organizations
children.content:
### `async list_organizations(*, page_num=1, items_per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /orgs?pageNum={N}&itemsPerPage={N}`.

### `async get_organization(org_id: str) -> Dict`
`GET /orgs/{org_id}`.

children.title: Projects (Groups)
children.content:
### `async list_projects(*, page_num=1, items_per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /groups?pageNum={N}&itemsPerPage={N}`.

### `async get_project(project_id) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{project_id}`.

### `async create_project(name, org_id, with_default_alerts_settings=True) -> Dict`
`POST /groups` — body `{name, orgId, withDefaultAlertsSettings}`.

### `async delete_project(project_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /groups/{project_id}`.

children.title: Clusters
children.content:
### `async list_clusters(project_id) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{project_id}/clusters`.

### `async get_cluster(project_id, cluster_name) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{project_id}/clusters/{cluster_name}`.

### `async create_cluster(project_id, name, cluster_type='REPLICASET', provider_settings=None, num_shards=1, mongo_db_major_version='7.0') -> Dict`
`POST /groups/{project_id}/clusters` — body built by `helpers.utils.build_cluster_payload`. Defaults to AWS US_EAST_1 / M10 / MongoDB 7.0 when `provider_settings` is omitted.

### `async modify_cluster(project_id, cluster_name, patch) -> Dict`
`PATCH /groups/{project_id}/clusters/{cluster_name}` — partial update with the `patch` dict.

### `async delete_cluster(project_id, cluster_name) -> Dict`
`DELETE /groups/{project_id}/clusters/{cluster_name}` — irreversible teardown.

children.title: Database Users
children.content:
### `async list_database_users(project_id, items_per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{project_id}/databaseUsers`.

### `async create_database_user(project_id, username, password, database_name='admin', roles=None, scopes=None) -> Dict`
`POST /groups/{project_id}/databaseUsers` — body built by `helpers.utils.build_database_user_payload`. Default role is `readWriteAnyDatabase`.

### `async delete_database_user(project_id, username, database_name='admin') -> Dict`
`DELETE /groups/{project_id}/databaseUsers/{database_name}/{username}`.

children.title: Network Access List
children.content:
### `async list_network_access(project_id) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{project_id}/accessList`.

### `async add_network_access(project_id, entries) -> Dict`
`POST /groups/{project_id}/accessList` — body is a JSON **array** of entries (Atlas convention). Each entry:
`{"cidrBlock": "10.0.0.0/24", "comment": "office-vpn"}` or `{"ipAddress": "1.2.3.4", "comment": "bastion"}` or `{"awsSecurityGroup": "sg-…", "comment": "…"}`.

children.title: Snapshots · Alerts
children.content:
### `async list_snapshots(project_id, cluster_name) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{project_id}/clusters/{cluster_name}/backup/snapshots` — Cloud Backup snapshot index for a single cluster.

### `async list_alerts(project_id, status=None, page_num=1, items_per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{project_id}/alerts` — optional `status` filter (`OPEN`, `CLOSED`, `TRACKING`).

children.title: API Keys · Billing
children.content:
### `async list_api_keys(org_id, page_num=1, items_per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /orgs/{org_id}/apiKeys` — list org-level programmatic keys. Read-only here; key provisioning is intentionally out of scope to avoid privilege-escalation surfaces.

### `async list_invoices(org_id, page_num=1, items_per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /orgs/{org_id}/invoices` — list org billing invoices.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
MongoDBAtlasError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── MongoDBAtlasAuthError                # 401 / 403
├── MongoDBAtlasBadRequestError          # 400
├── MongoDBAtlasNotFoundError            # 404  (alias: MongoDBAtlasNotFound)
├── MongoDBAtlasConflictError            # 409  (duplicate cluster name, etc.)
├── MongoDBAtlasRateLimitError           # 429  (retry_after_s)
├── MongoDBAtlasServerError              # 5xx
└── MongoDBAtlasNetworkError             # transport (DNS, timeout, refused)
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff honouring `Retry-After` header when present; otherwise `_BACKOFF_BASE_S * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s). Retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `MongoDBAtlasNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            — logged as warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, AUTHENTICATED)
other   → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, AUTHENTICATED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `public_key` or `private_key` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Atlas shows the private key only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Atlas — API key lacks the role | In Atlas → Access Manager → API Keys, grant `Organization Owner` (or `Organization Project Creator` + per-project `Project Owner`). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 401 — Digest credentials wrong or key revoked | Verify `public_key` / `private_key` exactly. Atlas Digest fails silently for typos. Re-generate the key if needed. |
| Persistent 401 after correct keys | Caller IP not on the API key's access list | In Atlas → API Key detail → Access List, add the egress IP of the host running the connector. |
| `mongodb_atlas.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Atlas per-minute quota) | The client retries honouring `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_cluster` / `get_project` | Wrong project_id or cluster_name | Verify with `list_projects` / `list_clusters`; Atlas IDs are 24-char hex from the URL. |
| `MongoDBAtlasConflictError` on `create_cluster` | Cluster name already in use in this project | Pick a different name or `delete_cluster` first (irreversible). |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'MongoDBAtlasNotFound'` | Older callers using legacy name | Already fixed — `MongoDBAtlasNotFound = MongoDBAtlasNotFoundError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Versioned Accept rejected by Atlas | Atlas deprecated the version date set in `api_version` | Bump `api_version` in install field to the current supported date (Atlas publishes changes at https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/reference/api-versioning). |
| Wire-protocol queries needed (data plane) | Atlas Admin API doesn't expose data plane | Use `pymongo` / `motor` with the connection string from `get_cluster(...)['connectionStrings']`. This connector is admin-only by design. |
